pages.raw_text:
An enthusiastic meeting of the
joint committee from the Central
Labor council and the Township
Good Government club was held
last night in the rooms of the
Equal Suffrage association.


In the words of the chairman,
r. B. Martin, the general feeling
of the committee was that Thank
God, the day of deliverance from
ring domination is at last at
hand"


Candidates were considered and
the list of primary preferences
completed with one or two excep-
tons. This list will include both
republicans and democrats.


For such offices as no satisfac-
tory candidates have applied other
material will be sought and COM
mittee to complete the list and in
duce the right kind of men to run
was named.


1t was agreed that the good gov
eminent rally Saturday afternoon
at 2 o'clock at Mantle Park should
be made big affair. Farmers are
coming in from every corner OF
Spokane county, and as Saturday
afternoon Is a holiday a large rep.
resentation of the forces of or
ganized labor Is expected.
Harry A. Jarvis, president of the
Cenaral Labor council, has been
asked to preside. The committee
on speakers was authorized to in
vite r. B. Martin and George l.
Reed to speak on behalf of the
farmers, d. G. Coates and A. w
| SwensOn on behalf Of labor ant
Mrs. Minnie J. Reynolds on behalf
of the cause of equal suffrage
Judge Poindexter was also invited
to be present.


| A committee on arrangement:
consisting of Mrs. Hutton, w. J
| Coates and r. b. Martin was ap
pointed to look after further de
tails of the meeting. Frank Goult
of the cooks and walters is thu
general secretary OF the Joint com
mittee.

and Good Government club ha
been asked to meet in the assem-
bly, room of the chamber at 10 a
